name: rust-import-organizer
description: Organize, sort, group, and auto-import Rust use statements

enumDescriptions: Three fixed groups: std / external / local
Keep your existing blank-line groups; only sort within each group
Use importOrder to define fully custom groups

rust-import-organizer.sortAlphabetically.description: Sort imports alphabetically within each group.

rust-import-organizer.blankLineBetweenGr.description: Insert a blank line between import groups.

rust-import-organizer.collapseSingleImpo.description: Collapse grouped imports filtered to a single item (e.g. use std::path::{Path} → use std::path::Path).

rust-import-organizer.removeUnused.description: Remove unused imports. Disable to only sort and group without removing anything.

rust-import-organizer.enableAutoImport.description: Auto-import unresolved symbols when running 'Organize + Auto Import'. Requires Rust Analyzer.

rust-import-organizer.organizeOnSave.description: Automatically organize imports when saving a Rust file.

rust-import-organizer.importOrder.markdownDescription:
Ordered list of module prefixes that define custom import groups. Only used when `groupImports` is `"custom"`.

Special tokens: `"std"` (matches std/core/alloc), `"crate"` (matches crate::/super::/self::), `"*"` (catch-all).

Example: `["std", "tokio", "axum", "*", "crate"]`

enumDescriptions: Mixed in with regular imports by module category (default)
Own group at the very top, before std
Own group at the very bottom, before cfg
rust-import-organizer.pubUsePlacement.description: Where pub use re-exports are placed in the output.
